metadata.description: Per-epoch instrumented training measuring information-theoretic mechanism of PRMP

examples.output: {"aggregated_results": {"standard": {"mean_test_rmse": 0.587439, "std_test_rmse": 0.00466, "mean_best_val_rmse": 0.592773, "num_params": 83585}, "prmp": {"mean_test_rmse": 0.583108, "std_test_rmse": 0.005209, "mean_best_val_rmse": 0.590142, "num_params": 117377, "rel_improve_pct": 0.7373}, "no_subtract": {"mean_test_rmse": 0.586801, "std_test_rmse": 0.005417, "mean_best_val_rmse": 0.591906, "num_params": 133249, "rel_improve_pct": 0.1086}}, "mechanism_diagnosis": {"information_filtering_supported": true, "mi_residual_vs_raw_ratio": 1.2993, "prediction_r2_final": -0.9406, "gradient_angle_trend": "orthogonal", "weight_growth_rate": 1.0066}, "conclusion": "PRMP achieves +0.74% relative RMSE change vs standard. No-subtract variant: +0.11%. Information filtering IS supported: MI(residual,target)/MI(raw,target) = 1.2993 > 1.0, meaning residuals carry more task-relevant information than raw features. Prediction MLP final R2 = -0.9406. Task vs prediction gradient angle: orthogonal. Prediction MLP weight growth: 1.01x from init to final."}
